Arts and Social Studies Library, Cardiff University

The Mackworth and Aylward music collections, formerly owned by Cardiff Central (Public) Library [GB-CDp], are now held at Special Collections and Archives, Arts and Social Studies Library, Cardiff University.

The significant and much referenced Register of St Osmund (or Old Register, early 13th century) which contains the earliest Customary of the Use of Salisbury (a source with considerable musical instructions, but not notation) has been moved permanently from the Wiltshire and Swindon History Centre (GB-CHIhc) to Salisbury Cathedral Archive (GB-SBca).

University of Wales Trinity Saint David - Lampeter campus

University of Wales, Lampeter was a university in Lampeter, Wales. Founded in 1822, and incorporated by royal charter in 1828, it was the oldest degree awarding institution in Wales, with limited degree awarding powers since 1852. It was a self-governing college of the University of Wales from 1972 until its merger (under its 1828 charter) with Trinity University College in 2010 to form the University of Wales Trinity Saint David.

The Bedford Estates office at Woburn Abbey in the UK is primarily a legal depository, but holds an important archive, the Bedford Opera Papers. This is an extensive set of documents relating to the Royal Italian Opera in London at the end of the 18th century.
